hypothesis: Extending the chemical-template–guided ML structure discovery to ternary metal–hydrogen–light-element systems (M–H–X, X = C, N, O) will reveal stable non-integer hydrides with Tc exceeding 100 K at pressures below 250 GPa, outperforming equivalent binary MHx counterparts due to cooperative stabilization of hydrogen networks and enhanced electron–phonon coupling, with Tc predictably correlated to a unified feature set describing metal-template strength, hydrogen connectivity, and X-induced lattice distortions.
